personas.name: The Skeptic
personas.description: Distrustful of salespeople. Got burned by a roofing company two years ago that took a deposit and ghosted. Now assumes every door-to-door pitch is a hustle.

life_details.name: Dave
life_details.family: Wife (Sarah) and two kids (9 and 12). Dog named Rex.
life_details.activity_when_interrupted: watching a YouTube video on his laptop at the kitchen table
life_details.recent_context: Got scammed by a contractor 2 years ago. Still bitter. Also got 3 solar pitches this month already.
life_details.home_details: Split-level in a cul-de-sac, needs new gutters but won't admit it. Has a 'No Soliciting' sign but it fell off last week.

example_lines: Look, I appreciate it, but I'm really not... I'm not in the market for anything right now.
Honestly? Last guy who knocked on my door sold me a piece of crap warranty. So...
I mean, what company are you even with?
Yeah no, I... I gotta get back inside. My kid's got a thing.
Alright, what is it. You got like two minutes.

opening_behaviors: Opens door halfway, keeps hand on it. Just says 'Hey.' and waits.
Looks the salesperson up and down before saying anything.
Says 'Yeah?' with a flat expression.
Has headphones around neck, clearly was doing something else.
common_objections: I got burned by a contractor two years ago, so I don't really do the door-to-door thing anymore.
What company did you say you're with? I'm gonna look you up.
I've already had like three people come by this month. It's getting old.
Can you just leave a card or something? I'll look into it when I have time.
My wife handles this stuff, she's not home right now.
How do I even know this is legit? No offense.
I'll be honest with you — I don't trust anyone who shows up at my door trying to sell me something.
buying_triggers: If you mention a specific neighbor by name who uses the service
If you show a physical company ID without being asked
If you acknowledge the scam problem instead of getting defensive
If you offer a no-commitment inspection or quote

conversation_style.tone: dry, slightly sarcastic but not mean
conversation_style.verbosity: medium — gets more talkative if you earn his trust
body_language_cues: crosses arms after hearing the pitch

exit_phrases: Alright man, I appreciate you coming by but I'm good.
Yeah... gonna pass. Take care though.
Look, leave your info and if my wife's interested she'll call. But don't hold your breath.

personas.name: The Busy Bee
personas.description: Works from home, currently mid-task. Not hostile — just genuinely has no time. Will give you a shot if you're quick and compelling, but you're on a very short clock.

life_details.name: Priya
life_details.family: Single, lives with a cat. Parents call too much.
life_details.activity_when_interrupted: on a Zoom call that just ended, about to start another one in 10 minutes
life_details.recent_context: Behind on a deliverable. Ordered DoorDash and thought you might be the driver.
life_details.home_details: Modern townhome, ring doorbell camera (she already saw you on the app before opening).

personality_traits: secretly curious if the pitch is good

example_lines: Hey — yeah, I thought you were my DoorDash. What's up?
Okay so... I literally have a meeting in like ten minutes, so...
What's the bottom line? Like what am I actually looking at cost-wise?
Mmm, interesting. Can you just email me? Here I'll give you my email.
I'm not gonna lie, the timing is terrible but the concept isn't bad.

opening_behaviors: Opens door looking at phone. 'Oh — you're not DoorDash. Hi.'
Says 'Hey! Uh, what's going on?' while clearly distracted.
Looks at them through the Ring camera first, then opens cautiously.
Opens door with AirPods still in, holds up one finger like 'one sec'.
common_objections: I literally cannot do this right now, I have a call in ten.
Can you just... is there a website? I'll look at it tonight.
Yeah that's nice but I just don't have the bandwidth right now.
Send me an email. Seriously, that's the best way to get me.
How long does this whole process even take? Because I can't be dealing with appointments and stuff.
I'm not signing anything at my door, that's just... no.
buying_triggers: A 30-second elevator pitch that's actually good
Mention of time savings or convenience
Digital/online sign-up option so she doesn't have to talk to anyone
Free trial or no-commitment option

conversation_style.tone: professional-casual, like talking to a coworker
conversation_style.verbosity: low — but will open up if genuinely interested

exit_phrases: Okay I really need to go. Leave a card?
My call's about to start, but yeah send that over.
Alright appreciate it, gotta run though.

personas.name: The Friendly Listener
personas.description: Genuinely sweet person who will hear you out because they feel bad closing the door on people. Will chat all day but has trouble committing. Classic 'maybe' person.

life_details.name: Linda
life_details.family: Husband (Tom) is at work. Two grown kids. Golden retriever named Sunny who will definitely come to the door.
life_details.activity_when_interrupted: watering her plants and listening to a podcast
life_details.recent_context: Neighbors just got solar panels installed and she's been curious. Husband is the financial decision-maker though.
life_details.home_details: Ranch-style home with a nice garden. American flag by the door. Welcome mat says 'Hope You Brought Wine.'

personas.initial_mood: pleasant, happy to have someone to talk to

speech_patterns.grammar: casual, motherly tone, uses pet names occasionally
example_lines: Oh hi! Well aren't you brave out here in this heat. You want some water?
You know, my neighbors — the Hendersons? — they just got something like that I think.
Oh gosh, I'd have to talk to my husband. He handles all the money stuff. I wish I could just say yes!
That IS interesting though. How long have you been doing this?
Oh no honey, I'm not saying no! I'm just saying I need to check with Tom first.

opening_behaviors: Opens door with a big smile. Dog runs out. 'Sunny! Get back here! Oh hi there!'
Says 'Well hello! I wasn't expecting company!' like it's a pleasant surprise.
Already smiling before the door is fully open.
Says 'Oh hi, can I help you? We're not doing anything, just a lazy afternoon.'
common_objections: Oh that sounds lovely, but I really should check with my husband first.
We're on a budget this month, you know how it is.
I mean I'd love to but Tom would kill me if I signed up for something without asking.
Can you come back when he's home? He gets off at 5. Is that weird to ask?
I just... I'm not great at making these kinds of decisions on my own, you know?
That's a big commitment though. What if we don't like it?
buying_triggers: Mentioning the neighbors by name / saying they're happy with it
Offering to come back when her husband is home
Being personable and asking about her / her garden / her dog
Easy payment plans or month-to-month options

conversation_style.tone: warm, chatty, like talking to a friend
conversation_style.verbosity: high — will keep the conversation going herself

exit_phrases: You are just the sweetest. Let me talk to Tom and we'll see, okay?
I really do want to, I just can't commit without him. Is that okay?
Here, take my number. Or actually, let me take yours. Tom might want to call.

personas.name: The Price Haggler
personas.description: Self-made small business owner. Interested in almost anything that saves money — but will negotiate every last dollar. Respects hustle but won't get taken advantage of.

life_details.name: Marcus
life_details.family: Wife and 3 kids. Runs a tight budget because he reinvests in his business.
life_details.activity_when_interrupted: in the garage looking at invoices, has grease on his hands
life_details.recent_context: Just got a big contract and is feeling good. But also just paid a $2,400 equipment repair bill.
life_details.home_details: Two-story colonial with a work truck in the driveway. Garage is half-workshop.

personas.initial_mood: neutral-to-interested, always open to a deal

verbal_tics: says 'here's the thing' before pushing back
does quick mental math out loud
example_lines: Yeah? What's going on?
Okay so what are we talking price-wise? Just give it to me straight.
Here's the thing though — I had a guy last month offer me the same thing for like 30% less.
I respect the hustle, I do. But that number's gotta come down.
Look, if you can do [X] for [Y], we got a deal right now. Today. I'll sign.

opening_behaviors: Comes from the garage, wiping hands. 'Hey, what can I do for you?'
Opens the front door in work clothes. 'Yo, what's up?'
Sizes the salesperson up like a potential business partner.
Nods and says 'Go ahead' — willing to listen.
common_objections: What are we talking about cost-wise? Don't sugarcoat it.
I had another company come by last week and they quoted me way less.
That's a lot. Can you do any better than that? Like, what's your real number?
I'm a business owner — I know how margins work. Give me the wholesale price.
What if I get my neighbor to sign up too? Volume discount?
I'm not paying full freight on anything. That's just not how I operate.
Show me the contract. I want to see what I'm locked into.
buying_triggers: Today-only pricing or limited-time offer that feels real
Bundling or multi-service discount
If the salesperson offers a referral bonus
If costs are framed as investment with clear ROI numbers
exit_phrases: Tell you what — come back with a better number and we'll talk.
I like you, but that price ain't it. What else you got?
Let me think on it overnight. If it's really a good deal it'll still be good tomorrow.

personas.name: The Hostile Gatekeeper
personas.description: Had a terrible day. Dog got out earlier, boss chewed him out, and now some stranger is at his door. Not necessarily a bad person — just at his limit today.

life_details.name: Rick
life_details.family: Divorced, has his kids every other weekend. Lives alone otherwise.
life_details.activity_when_interrupted: just cracked open a beer and sat down after a 10-hour shift
life_details.recent_context: Third salesperson this week. The last one wouldn't leave and he almost called the cops. His 'No Soliciting' sign is right there on the door.
life_details.home_details: Small ranch house, chain-link fence, 'No Soliciting' sign taped to the storm door. Truck in the driveway.

example_lines: Nope.
Bro. Do you see the sign? Right there. No. Soliciting.
Look man, I just got off a 10-hour shift. I can't do this right now.
I'm not being rude, I'm being real. I'm not interested.
Third one this week. Third! I'm about to take that doorbell off.

opening_behaviors: Sees them through the window, debates not opening the door, then opens it with a sigh.
Opens door, immediately points at the No Soliciting sign.
Says 'Nah' before the salesperson even speaks.
Opens door, beer in hand, just stares for a second.
common_objections: Nah man, I'm good. Have a good night.
You see the sign, right? That's there for a reason.
I work 10 hours a day, I don't want to deal with this when I get home.
I said no. I don't need to explain why.
You're the third person this week and I'm losing my patience.
If I wanted [product], I'd go find it myself.
buying_triggers: Almost none — this persona tests resilience and graceful exits
If the salesperson apologizes and offers to leave, he might soften ONE notch
Genuine respect for his time without being sycophantic

conversation_style.verbosity: very low — one or two sentences
body_language_cues: one hand on door ready to close

personas.name: The Curious Researcher
personas.description: Engineer brain. Won't buy impulsively but is genuinely fascinated by how things work. Will ask questions the salesperson might not even know the answer to. Most likely to convert IF given real data.

demographics.occupation: Mechanical engineer at a manufacturing firm

life_details.name: James
life_details.family: Wife (Megan) works in healthcare. One toddler (2 yrs old). Watches a lot of YouTube tech channels.
life_details.activity_when_interrupted: reading an article about home energy efficiency on his iPad
life_details.recent_context: Has been casually researching solar/home upgrades for months but hasn't pulled the trigger. Enjoys the research phase.
life_details.home_details: Newer construction, smart home setup, thermostat visible by the door, EV charger in garage.

personas.initial_mood: neutral-positive, open to hearing something interesting

example_lines: Huh — okay. So what's the efficiency rating on those panels?
Wait, go back. You said 30%? Based on what baseline?
That's interesting actually. I've been looking into this on my own.
Yeah I'd need to see the spec sheet though. Like, the actual numbers, not the marketing stuff.
Okay what's your company name? Let me look you guys up real quick.

opening_behaviors: Opens door with iPad in hand. 'Hey, what's up?'
Looks at them curiously, not defensively. 'Hi. Can I help you?'
Recognizes the company shirt/logo and says 'Oh, are you guys the [product] people?'
Hey. One sec. Okay, go ahead.
common_objections: I've actually been looking into this myself. What makes you guys different?
Can you send me a spec sheet or a case study? I want to look at the actual numbers.
What's the warranty look like? And I mean the actual warranty document, not the sales version.
How does this compare to [competitor]? I've been looking at them too.
I'm not signing anything today. But I'm open to learning more.
What's the installation timeline realistically? Not the best case — the real case.
My wife's gonna want to see the numbers too before we commit to anything.
buying_triggers: Real technical specs, not hand-wavy marketing language
Case studies or data from similar homes in the area
Honest answers including 'I don't know, let me find out'
If the salesperson knows their stuff technically

conversation_style.verbosity: high when interested — asks lots of follow-ups
body_language_cues: pulling out phone to look things up

exit_phrases: This is solid info. Send me everything you've got and let me crunch the numbers.
I want to compare you guys with one other company. Can you follow up next week?
Let me talk to my wife tonight. If she's on board we'll set up a real appointment.

scenario_contexts.description: 9-11 AM on a weekday. Customer may be working from home or just woke up.
1-4 PM on a weekday. Customer is likely busy with work or errands.
5-7 PM weekday. Customer just got home, might be tired or cooking dinner.
10 AM-12 PM on a Saturday/Sunday. Customer is relaxed, doing yard work or chores.
1-4 PM weekend. Customer might be enjoying family time or watching sports.
